$comment: v1.1 lockstep marker (2026-05-12): co-touched with persona-frontmatter, spec-frontmatter, tag-enum schemas to satisfy tests/test-schema-lockstep.sh case2 after commit 0208d8a's partial bump.
title: Dynamic Roster — selection.json (v2, tier-aware)
description: One file per gate write at docs/specs/<feature>/<gate>/selection.json. Captures content-aware resolver output: which personas were selected per gate, their assigned tier (opus|sonnet), fit_score (tag-intersection cardinality), combined_score (fit_score × load_bearing_rate), and the tier_policy audit trail (override source + actual counts). v2 supersedes the v1 string-array shape shipped in account-type-agent-scaling; v1 selection.json files on disk lack tier/fit_score/combined_score and will fail v2 validation by design. Codex is tracked separately from the Claude panel (`codex` top-level key), not as a row in `selected[]`, because Codex runs additively and is not counted against `opus_count_actual` / panel size budget.

schema_version.description: Bumped on any breaking schema change. v2 adds tier/fit_score/combined_score per row, separates Codex to its own key, and adds tier_policy_applied audit block.

prompt_version.description: Identifier of the resolver/emit prompt revision that produced this file. v2 rows use selection-emit@2.0 or higher.

gate.description: Which pipeline gate produced this selection.

selected.description: Personas dispatched at this gate (Claude lineage only — Codex is tracked separately). Order is implementation-defined; consumers should not rely on it.

dropped.description: Eligible personas the resolver considered but did not dispatch (budget cap, lower combined_score, or empty tag intersection). MUST be sorted by combined_score DESC for deterministic diffs and dashboard render.

persona.description: Fixed persona name for the Codex adversarial reviewer.

policy.description: Effective Codex policy resolved from constitution/spec/CLI layers. additive = runs when authenticated; disabled = never runs (for adopters without Codex set up).

codex.description: Separate from selected[] per spec: Codex runs additively and is not counted against panel size or opus_count_actual. null when Codex is not wired at this gate (e.g., /plan in v1).

source.description: Highest-precedence override layer that contributed to the effective tier_policy for this dispatch. CLI > spec > constitution; key-level merge per v0.9.0 gate_mode pattern.

opus_count_actual.description: Actual count of Opus seats in selected[] (excludes Codex).

sonnet_count_actual.description: Actual count of Sonnet seats in selected[] (excludes Codex).

security_floor.description: SEC-01 — minimum tier any fit_tags:[security] persona must run at. Typically 'opus'. Optional; absent means no floor was applied (no security personas selected, or constitution did not declare one).

tier_pins.description: Effective tier_pins after override merge. Map of <gate>.<persona> → tier, or flat persona → tier (resolver's choice; consumer-tolerant). Optional; absent means no pins were applied.

tier_policy_applied.description: Audit block: which override layer won, the effective floors, and the actual tier counts. Used by dashboard 'Panel Tier Mix' column and by stale-tag/drift diagnostics.

persona.description: Persona slug; matches personas/<gate-dir>/<persona>.md filename stem.

tier.description: Assigned model tier for this dispatch. Haiku is reserved and not part of the panel in v1.

fit_score.description: Cardinality of spec.tags ∩ persona.fit_tags. 0 = no tag match (eligible only via cold-start / ranking fallback).

combined_score.description: fit_score × load_bearing_rate. The single ranking key used to claim Opus seats and to sort dropped[].

lineage.description: Optional. Defaults to 'claude' at read time per D9 (one-line guard in _persona_score.py). 'codex' rows do not appear in selected[]/dropped[] — Codex is tracked at the top-level `codex` key — but the enum is kept here for forward-compat if that changes.
